title: Grounds for Disciplinary Action Against Seafarers and their Penalties.
content: 52 Commission by a seafarer of any of the offenses enumerated below shall be a ground for 53 disciplinary action for which the corresponding penalties shall be imposed. 54 55 I. GRAVE OFFENSES. The following acts shall be considered as grave offenses which 56 shall merit the penalty of permanent disqualification from participation in the overseas 57 employment program: 58 59 a. Possession, sale, transport, distribution, delivery, supply, or use of prohibited 60 drugs, narcotics, or other drug-related contraband; 61 62 48 b. Sexual assault; 1 2 c. Rape; 3 4 d. Murder; 5 6 e. Acts of terrorism; or 7 8 f. Other heinous crimes. 9 10 II. SERIOUS OFFENSES. Serious offenses are punishable by One (1) Year and One (1) 11 Day to Two (2) Years suspension from participation in the overseas employment program: 12 13 a. Submitting, furnishing, or using false material information or documents or any 14 form of gross misrepresentation for the purpose of job application or employment; 15 16 b. Willfully making a false statement, report, certification or document for personal 17 gain or with intent to mislead or defraud the company or the authorities; 18 19 c. Commission of other acts amounting to a felony or crime involving moral turpitude 20 punishable by the laws of the Philippines, the flag state or the port state committed 21 during employment, including but not limited to: 22 23 i. Gun-running or illegal/unauthorized possession of firearms, ammunition, 24 explosives and/or related items thereto; or 25 26 ii. Possession of child pornographic materials. 27 28 d. Unjustified breach of employment contract by the seafarer; 29 30 e. Gross violation of LMA policies and regulations; 31 32 f. Deserting or attempting to desert; 33 34 g. Physically assaulting a superior officer, crew, passenger or other persons with 35 business onboard the ship; 36 37 h. Grave abuse of authority causing serious harm, injury, illness or death to any 38 person on board; 39 40 i. Inciting mutiny or malicious destruction of ship’s property or any activity which will 41 endanger the safety of the ship and its crew; 42 43 j. Drunkenness, such as being drunk or intoxicated while on duty, creating trouble 44 on board due to alcohol intoxication, or failure to perform assigned jobs due to 45 alcohol intoxication; 46 47 k. Connivance with or coddling of a stowaway; or 48 49 l. Conviction for violation of the Anti-Violence Against Women and Children Act (R.A. 50 No. 9262) or any other relevant laws involving gender-based violence. 51 52 III. LESS SERIOUS OFFENSES. Less serious offenses are punishable by Six (6) Months 53 to One (1) Year suspension from participation in the overseas employment program: 54 55 a. Unjustified refusal to join ship after all employment and travel documents have 56 been duly approved by the appropriate government agencies; 57 58 b. Violation of the Code of Discipline for Seafarers; 59 60 c. Other forms of misrepresentation relative to the overseas employment of the 61 seafarer; 62 49 d. Smuggling or violation of any customs rules and regulations of the Philippines and 1 of foreign ports, such as: 2 3 1. Smuggling any taxable item; 4 2. Abetting or conniving with others to commit smuggling; 5 3. Misdeclaration of or failing to declare articles leading to their seizure and fine 6 to the ship; 7 4. Misdeclaration of or failing to declare articles leading to their seizure but the 8 ship is not implicated; 9 5. Possession of pornographic materials other than child pornography leading 10 to their confiscation and fine to the ship; and 11 6. Any other violation of customs rules and regulations which implicates the 12 ship; 13 14 e. Advising, assisting or persuading another to desert; 15 16 f. Abandoning or leaving one’s post or duty without being properly relieved; 17 18 g. Leaving the ship without permission from responsible officers during or outside 19 working hours; 20 21 h. Entrusting to others assigned duties without authority of a superior officer; 22 23 i. Sleeping on post while on duty; 24 25 j. Insubordination or any act of disobedience to lawful orders of a superior officer; 26 27 k. Attempting to assault a superior officer, crew, passenger or other persons with 28 business at the ship; 29 30 l. Behaving with disrespect towards a superior officer; 31 32 m. Insulting a superior officer by words or deeds; 33 34 n. Inciting another to commit insubordination; 35 36 o. Creating trouble outside the ship’s premises that unjustly implicates the ship; 37 38 p. Any form of gambling, including online gambling, that results in fighting or any 39 incident as to upset the harmonious relationship on board the ship or any other 40 form of gambling, including online gambling, which is not purely recreational; 41 42 q. Violation of company policies and regulations for: 43 44 1. Pilferage or theft of ship’s store or cargo; 45 2. Pilferage or theft of ship’s property, of crew or passengers or other persons 46 with business at the ship; 47 3. Embezzlement of ship’s funds; 48 4. Unauthorized disposal of ship’s properties for personal gain; 49 5. Any act of dishonesty with intention to defraud the company; 50 6. Gross negligence and failure to observe proper storage and cargo handling 51 procedures resulting in delay of ships and/or damage to cargo; 52 7. Failure to observe and comply with the regulation on non-manifested 53 baggage, parcels, and cargo on board; 54 8. Failure to observe regulations on expiration of shore liberty; 55 9. Being left behind by the ship in any port without justifiable reason; 56 10. Disorderly conduct and/or disrespect towards passengers or other persons; 57 11. Immorality so as to cast aspersion on the good name of the ship and the 58 company; or 59 12. Inflicting harm or injury to others. 60 61 r. Incompetence and inefficiency of a seafarer; 62 50 s. Concerted action to breach approved contracts; 1 2 t. Any activity which tends to destroy the harmonious relationship between and 3 among the ship’s crew and/or the company; 4 5 u. Grave abuse of authority which disturbs the harmonious relationship of the ship’s 6 crew; 7 8 v. Any other case of abuse of authority; 9 10 w. For gross misbehavior prejudicial to good order and discipline; 11 12 x. Gross negligence resulting in damage, loss, spoilage or deterioration of the ship’s 13 stocks and property; 14 15 y. Any other case as to cast aspersion on the good name of the company and the 16 ship; 17 18 z. Violation to observe safety and environmental rules/ regulations; 19 20 aa. Failure to comply with the drug and alcohol policy of the company; 21 22 bb. Embezzlement of monies and/or properties of a fellow worker entrusted for delivery 23 to kin or relatives in the Philippines; 24 25 cc. Failure to refund the cost of his/her repatriation advanced by the principal/employer 26 or licensed manning agency where termination of employment was due to his/her 27 own fault; 28 29 dd. Failure or refusal by the master mariner to present employment records, such as 30 payroll and daily time records, pay slips, or other documents, such as medical 31 records, that belong or pertain to the seafarer and that is in the possession of the 32 master mariner, when required by the individual seafarer, the Secretary of the 33 DMW, the Secretary of the DOLE, their duly authorized representatives, or by the 34 duly authorized representative of a recognized organization provided that the 35 maximum fine that may be imposed for this offense shall not exceed Php 36 1,000,000.00, pursuant to R.A. No. 12021 and its IRR; or 37 38 ee. Unjustified refusal to be repatriated in case of mandatory repatriation in 39 accordance with the declaration of the Philippine government. 40 41 42 Complaints 43 44
